study_group_representative.quote: The potential contact-persons identified in this study tended to include more working populations and students because those who have no mobile phone are more likely to be very young or elderly people who are hard to be traced by this smart contact tracing technology.

intervention_and_participants_defined.quote: identify 627,386 potential contact-persons. Information on self-monitoring and self-quarantine was provided via SMS, and severe acute respiratory syndrome coronavirus 2 (SARS-CoV-2) tests were offered for symptomatic contacts.

outcome_assessed_for_60pct.quote: we used the National Health Insurance Claims data to track the health status of all subjects with potential contact.

follow_up_length_reported.quote: information on respiratory syndrome or pneumonia cases was ascertained by linkage with the big National Health Insurance claim database from January 31, 2020, to March 10, 2020.

outcome_assessors_blinded.quote: National Health Insurance claims big data were linked, to follow-up on the outcome related to COVID-19 among those who were hospitalized due to pneumonia and advised to undergo screening for SARS-CoV-2.

outcome_definition_objective_precise.quote: subjects with at least one outpatient visit with ICD-10 (The International Statistical Classification of Diseases and Related Health Problems, 10th Revision) codes (“J00” to “J11”) were identified as having respiratory syndrome. The subjects who had pneumonia were identified by ICD-10 codes (“J12-” to “J18”).

important_prognostic_factors_accounted_f.quote: The age-standardized rates between the two groups were calculated.

analysis_described_and_effect_quantified.quote: The age-standardized rates between the two groups were calculated. The Breslow and Day method [5] was used to calculate 95% confidence intervals.
